records.ety: [F. environner, fr. environ about, thereabout; pref. en- (L. in) + OF. viron circle, circuit, fr. OF. & F. virer to turn, LL. virare to turn up and down, topsy-turvy. Cf. Veer.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Environed (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Environing.]

defs: To surround; to encompass; to encircle; to hem in; to be round about; to involve or envelop.
quotes: Dwelling in a pleasant glade, With mountains round about environed.
Environed he was with many foes.
Environ me with darkness whilst I write.
Lord Godfrey's eye three times environ goes.
